text.title: Difficult Conversations Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: STATE opening ends 'You see that this is a problem, right?' — listener reads it as a trap.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused genuine inquiry with rhetorical confirmation.
antipattern.fix: Replace with genuine open question ('Can you help me understand?').

antipattern.symptom: STATE opening runs 200 words; listener disengages by sentence 8.
antipattern.root-cause: Author packed full case into opening.
antipattern.fix: Cap STATE at 60s spoken (3-5 sentences); move detail into AMPP listening phase.

antipattern.symptom: Author reads DESC script aloud in a 1:1; voice sounds robotic; safety drops.
antipattern.fix: Use STATE live; reserve DESC for async written boundary-setting.

antipattern.symptom: Conversation ends with 'we'll do better' — next week the same conflict resurfaces.
